opp.name: Eversana OppS2C - Mar'25
opp.next_step: 10/13/25 RH Invite Chamika to PLaN- determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled.
opp.ais_why:
• Two recorded discovery calls show clear interest in AI-driven AP automation; Zycus pitched cognitive OCR and AI invoice processing to fix Eversana's failing Xflow tool (5% PO-match versus 80% expected).
• Eversana's stakeholder explicitly framed AI as pivotal for innovation and reducing manual work, asking pointed questions about Microsoft Dynamics integration and IT effort needed for an AI procurement rollout.
• Qualified stage with two substantive 2025 calls; opportunity has stalled into nurture mode (Oct 13 next step questions if initiative is cancelled), limiting confidence despite strong AI receptiveness.

items.quote: Rep next step 13 Oct 2025 to determine if the initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled; no reply recorded

items.quote: We're beginning the process of seeing what other options are out there
CFO Kelly Baker is named Decision Maker but has never appeared on a call or activity
No committee, approval chain, or evaluation timeline captured on any call

sources: Avoma discovery call 28 May 2025, Jeannine Jenkins
Avoma discovery call 28 May 2025, Chamika McLean
metrics.narrative: Eversana quantified multiple operational impacts on the 28 May 2025 discovery call. Jeannine Jenkins (Corporate Controller) stated their Xflow AP tool achieves only a 5% automatic PO-to-invoice match versus the promised 80%, forcing the AP team to touch invoices 4-5 times when POs run out of funds. Chamika McLean (AP Manager) reported the AP inbox takes 30-50 vendor payment-status inquiries a day, and the Chief Accounting Officer spends hours weekly on manual three-way-match workarounds to allocate blanket-PO invoices across departments. The CFO has repeatedly questioned why per-clerk invoice throughput is low and has blocked AP headcount until the metric improves. Pain is concrete and role-attributed, but no single dollar-savings or ROI figure was ever stated, so the value case remains qualitative.

sources: Avoma calls 18 Mar 2025 and 28 May 2025, Chamika McLean
SF Next_Step__c 13 Oct 2025, no buyer reply
champion.narrative: Chamika McLean (AP Manager) was the original internal advocate: she initiated the 12 Mar 2025 touch with Zycus, brought Jeannine Jenkins into the 18 Mar 2025 intro call, drove the 28 May 2025 discovery session, and asked to schedule a live demo. Jeannine Jenkins (Corporate Controller) reinforced the pain and intent to reconvene. However, both went silent after 28 May 2025 and did not answer the rep's 13 Oct 2025 outreach; there is no reply on record in over a year. As of today (03 Jul 2026) there is no active champion and no multi-threading beyond the original AP/Finance pair. What developed in spring 2025 (genuine advocacy and detailed pain) has cooled to non-response, so any restart must re-recruit a champion, not assume one.

sources: Avoma discovery call 28 May 2025, Jeannine Jenkins on Xflow limitations
competition.narrative: There is no external rival vendor named in any field, call, or next step. The only named alternative is the incumbent Xflow AP module inside Microsoft Dynamics D365 F&O, which Jeannine Jenkins said on 28 May 2025 is failing (5% match, template-based OCR, foundational limitations they cannot work around). Eversana said they are 'beginning the process of seeing what other options are out there' but named no competitor, so Zycus is either sole-sourced or in a silent evaluation we cannot see. The wedge is clear: Merlin AI-driven capture in the high-90s, real-time PO-fund visibility, and invoice-time multi-workflow allocation directly answer the incumbent's failures. Whether a Coupa, SAP, Basware, or Tipalti is quietly in the frame is a genuine knowledge gap to close on re-engagement.

sources: Avoma discovery call 28 May 2025, Jeannine Jenkins
Avoma discovery call 28 May 2025, Chamika McLean
identify_pain.narrative: Eversana's pain is explicit and owned. Their Xflow AP module (part of Microsoft Dynamics D365 F&O), implemented over two years ago, delivers only a 5% automatic match instead of the promised 80%, so the AP team touches invoices 4-5 times when POs lack funds, with no real-time fund visibility until GL posting fails. The Chief Accounting Officer runs manual three-way matching to allocate blanket-PO spend across departments, and the CFO blocks AP headcount because throughput looks low, trapping an understaffed team in a vicious cycle. Chamika McLean also cited 30-50 daily vendor inquiries and payment-remittance format failures creating unapplied cash. Jeannine Jenkins and Chamika McLean are the confirmed pain owners.

sources: Avoma discovery call 28 May 2025, Chamika McLean question on integration and IT effort
paper_process.narrative: Contracting, legal, InfoSec, procurement channel, and any implementation-partner role have never been discussed. The only adjacent signal is Chamika McLean's 28 May 2025 question about D365 integration effort and IT resources, to which Rick Hunsinger replied Zycus uses an open-API methodology where 'IT does maybe 20% of the effort.' No MSA, SOW, or security review has begun. Given the deal is cold in Qualified, paper process is genuinely unknown; visibility would only come once a live evaluation restarts.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Kelly Baker, CFO, Decision Maker
SF OpportunityContactRole: Justin Stenz, SVP Finance, Evaluator (Primary)
Avoma call 28 May 2025, Jeannine Jenkins referencing the CFO
economic_buyer.narrative: Kelly Baker (Chief Financial Officer) is named as the Decision Maker on the Salesforce contact roles and is referenced on the 28 May 2025 call as the budget-holder pressuring AP: Jenkins said 'our CFO has repeatedly asked why is our invoice process per AP team member so low' and blocks headcount. So the economic buyer is IDENTIFIED by role and title, but she is a complete ghost: she has never appeared on a call, in a task, or in a next step. Justin Stenz (SVP Finance) is the PRIMARY contact and also unengaged. The gap is not identity but ACCESS. The move is to earn a direct conversation with Kelly Baker or Justin Stenz, since no commercial decision advances without finance sponsorship.

sources: Avoma introductory call 18 Mar 2025, Chamika McLean
decision_process.narrative: The buying process was never mapped. On 18 Mar 2025 Chamika McLean said understanding next steps 'probably would involve me having conversation with some additional individuals... and schedule a call that'll include those individuals,' signaling a committee, but that expanded group never materialized on any recorded call. No approval chain, procurement path, or evaluation timeline was captured, and the initiative may be stalled, postponed, or cancelled per the rep's own 13 Oct 2025 next step. The move that creates visibility is a direct re-engagement that asks Finance whether the AP-automation initiative is still funded and who owns the decision.

sources: Avoma introductory call 18 Mar 2025, Chamika McLean
Avoma discovery call 28 May 2025, Jeannine Jenkins and Chamika McLean
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer articulated clear functional criteria across both 2025 calls: AI-driven invoice capture beyond template OCR (high-90s recognition), real-time PO-fund visibility before approval, multi-workflow support for blanket and non-PO invoices with invoice-time department allocation, automated vendor payment-status handling, and low-IT-burden integration with the relevant stakeholder D365 F&O. Chamika McLean explicitly prized the 'guide-me' simplicity that reduces training burden. These are confirmed asks, but they were never formalized into a written requirements document or scored against alternatives, so the criteria are known but not yet an agreed evaluation framework.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — No external competitor has ever been named across any Salesforce field, call, or next step. The only alternative is the incumbent Xflow (Microsoft Dynamics D365 F&O AP module), which the buyer stated on 28 May 2025 is failing (5% match versus 80% promised) and has foundational limitations they cannot work around, so it is a displacement target, not a live threat. Whether Eversana is silently evaluating any S2C or AP-automation rival is unknown, a genuine knowledge gap to resolve on re-engagement.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 9 is carried by momentum 54 vs stage-expected 50; exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 54 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (9), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 54, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

critical_signals.text: The 28 May 2025 discovery call went well and the buyer asked for a live demo, but they went dark immediately after and have not answered a single outreach in 13 months.
No external rival has ever been named; the only alternative is the failing incumbent Xflow, so this is a sole-source displacement if it can be revived.
CFO Kelly Baker and SVP Finance Justin Stenz are both on record as the money owners but have never been met, leaving the deal with no path to power.
Pain is vivid but never quantified in dollars and no pricing or budget conversation has happened, so there is no business case a CFO could approve today.

confidence_signals.summary: Every signal points to a dormant deal: last buyer call 13 months ago, an unanswered October 2025 re-engagement, and recent outbound calls that could not connect. The strong 2025 pain and AI interest are real but stale.

north_star_verdict.math: Created and Qualified 18-21 Mar 2025, 469 days in Qualified. Last buyer call 28 May 2025 (13 months ago). Last Salesforce activity 08 May 2026 (56 days ago) was a rep-side 'Could Not Connect' outbound attempt, not buyer engagement. Close date 22 Jul 2026 is 19 days out. An eight-module S2C implementation cannot close in 19 days from a cold start. Cold for over a year, forced Off Track.
evidence: LastActivityDate 08 May 2026 was a 'Could Not Connect' outbound call to Matt Goyer (Nooks sequence), a rep-side touch, not buyer engagement
Last buyer call 28 May 2025 (Chamika McLean, Jeannine Jenkins), 13 months ago

Stage Qualified since 18 Mar 2025, 469 days with no forward movement
CloseDate slipped twice (06 Feb -> 30 Apr on 14 Jan 2026; 30 Apr -> 22 Jul on 14 Apr 2026) with no underlying buyer progress
Close date 22 Jul 2026, 19 days out, an impossible timeline for an eight-module S2C from zero momentum
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal frozen 13 months since the last buyer contact (28 May 2025). The October 2025 re-engagement attempt went unanswered, recent outbound calls to Matt Goyer could not connect, and the close date is 19 days out with the deal still in Qualified.

flags: Revive a dead deal with a value-first re-engagement to Chamika McLean and Jeannine Jenkins, referencing their own quantified May 2025 pain (5% match, 4-5 touches per invoice) rather than a generic check-in.

Break single-threading by reaching the two unengaged Finance leaders on record, Justin Stenz (SVP Finance, primary) and Kelly Baker (CFO, decision maker), since no purchase advances without Finance sponsorship.
Lead with the Merlin AI-AP proof point (high-90s capture, real-time PO-fund visibility) against the failing Xflow incumbent, the single differentiator most likely to re-open the conversation.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a cold, single-threaded early-stage deal with strong latent pain but no active buyer. The win levers are all about restarting the relationship, then multi-threading into Finance.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer call since 28 May 2025; the historical interest ('AI is critical and pivotal') has decayed with no re-engagement or Zycus-specific next step for over a year.

competitive_position.summary: No external competitor has ever been named across any Salesforce field, call, or next step. The only alternative is the incumbent Xflow (Microsoft Dynamics D365 F&O AP module), which the buyer stated on 28 May 2025 is failing (5% match versus 80% promised) and has foundational limitations they cannot work around, so it is a displacement target, not a live threat. Whether Eversana is silently evaluating any S2C or AP-automation rival is unknown, a genuine knowledge gap to resolve on re-engagement.

competitors.name: Xflow (Microsoft Dynamics D365 F&O AP module, incumbent)
competitors.quote: We have about a 5% match, PO to invoice, automatic match. When we implemented Xflow they said we would get about an 80% match. Two years later we're still at 5%. The system itself is a little antiquated, still using templates, the OCR is old technology, not using true AI. There are foundational limitations to Xflow that we don't believe we're going to be able to work around.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin AI-driven invoice capture (high-90s, no templates, autonomous vendor-inquiry handling) plus real-time PO-fund visibility and invoice-time blanket-PO allocation as the leap beyond Xflow's template-based, late-error-detection model, and prove low-IT D365 open-API integration.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus positioned the AI story well on both 2025 calls (Merlin cognitive OCR in the high-90s, autonomous vendor-inquiry handling, guide-me UX) and the buyer responded enthusiastically, calling AI 'pivotal.' The story landed; the problem is that no one has been in front of the buyer to keep it alive since May 2025.

items.quote: Basic OCR that's not using AI is just not as effective... AI can greatly innovate processes
items.criterion: AI-driven invoice capture beyond template OCR

items.quote: The approver doesn't even know if there's funds available on a PO when they approve an invoice, so we don't know until the very last step
We want to ensure that it can accommodate multiple workflows... allocate that to 4 different departments
Is there an integration required to get the data passed between Zycus and D365 and how much IT resources would an implementation require?

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Eversana's stated expectations map tightly to the Zycus S2C and AI-AP suite; the risk is not fit but a cold relationship, since none of these criteria have been re-confirmed since May 2025.

hard.ais_why:
• Two recorded discovery calls show clear interest in AI-driven AP automation; Zycus pitched cognitive OCR and AI invoice processing to fix Eversana's failing Xflow tool (5% PO-match versus 80% expected).
• Eversana's stakeholder explicitly framed AI as pivotal for innovation and reducing manual work, asking pointed questions about Microsoft Dynamics integration and IT effort needed for an AI procurement rollout.
• Qualified stage with two substantive 2025 calls; opportunity has stalled into nurture mode (Oct 13 next step questions if initiative is cancelled), limiting confidence despite strong AI receptiveness.
hard.next_step: 10/13/25 RH Invite Chamika to PLaN- determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled.
